ChannelFlags.brief: A collection of constants representing different in-game communication channel identifiers.
ChannelFlags.details: The ChannelFlags group defines a set of constant integer values, each corresponding to a specific communication channel used within the game system. These channels support various forms of player interaction and system communication, including general chat, clan messages, music, Qwest, prayer, and administrative channels such as immortal talk and auction. Organizing these identifiers into a dedicated group promotes clarity, consistency, and ease of maintenance when referencing communication pathways throughout the codebase. They are employed in message routing, permission checks, and channel-specific features to ensure proper segregation and management of in-game communication streams.
ChannelFlags.notes: The constants are organized sequentially, starting from zero, and are used consistently across messaging functions to identify channels. This organization supports modular handling of communication types and simplifies the addition of new channels in the future.
ChannelFlags.rationale: Having a centralized set of channel identifiers allows the system to efficiently manage multiple communication streams, enforce access controls, and maintain clear separation of message types. This structure enhances code readability, reduces errors, and facilitates future expansion of communication features.
ChannelFlags.name: ChannelFlags

CHAN_GOSSIP.brief: A constant that identifies the gossip communication channel within the system.
CHAN_GOSSIP.details: The constant CHAN_GOSSIP is initialized to zero and serves as an identifier for the gossip channel used in character communication. It is commonly passed as an argument to messaging functions like 'channel' to specify that the message should be broadcast via gossip. This constant helps differentiate gossip messages from other types of communication channels, enabling organized and context-aware message handling across the system.
CHAN_GOSSIP.notes: Since CHAN_GOSSIP is defined as zero, it may be used as a default or primary channel identifier, and its value aligns with the convention of using zero-based indexing for channel types.
CHAN_GOSSIP.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, named identifier for the gossip channel, simplifying code readability and maintenance by avoiding magic numbers and ensuring consistent usage across messaging functions.
CHAN_GOSSIP.name: CHAN_GOSSIP

usages.channels_8cc, void do_gossip(Character *: Serves as a constant parameter indicating that the message should be sent via the gossip channel, influencing the behavior of the 'channel' function.

CHAN_FLAME.brief: A constant that identifies the 'flame' communication channel type within the messaging system.
CHAN_FLAME.details: The constant CHAN_FLAME is used to represent the 'flame' channel, a specific communication pathway that characters can use to send messages. It is initialized with the value 1 and is utilized in functions such as 'channel' and 'do_flame' to specify the message type, enforce permission checks, and control message broadcasting within the system. By defining this constant, the code maintains clarity and consistency when referring to the flame channel, enabling easy identification and management of this particular communication mode.
CHAN_FLAME.notes: This constant is typically used as an argument in functions that handle message routing and permission validation for the flame channel, ensuring that messages are correctly categorized and access-controlled.
CHAN_FLAME.rationale: The CHAN_FLAME constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to identify and handle the flame communication channel, supporting thematic messaging and ensuring proper access restrictions within the system.
CHAN_FLAME.name: CHAN_FLAME

usages.channels_8cc, void do_flame(Character *c: Serves as a constant argument specifying the particular channel type (flame) to be used in the channel function call.
usages.channels_8cc, void channel(Character *ch: Represents the flame channel, used to check permissions and restrict message broadcasting accordingly.

CHAN_QWEST.brief: A constant that identifies the 'Qwest' communication channel within the system.
CHAN_QWEST.details: The constant CHAN_QWEST is used to represent the 'Qwest' communication channel in the application's messaging framework. It is initialized with the value 2 and is employed as an argument in functions like 'do_qwest' to specify that the message should be transmitted through the Qwest channel. This constant facilitates clear and consistent referencing of the Qwest channel across different parts of the code, enabling proper routing and handling of messages associated with this specific communication pathway.
CHAN_QWEST.notes: As a fixed identifier, CHAN_QWEST is stored as a compile-time constant to ensure efficiency and prevent accidental modification. Its usage pattern involves passing it to communication functions to specify the target channel.
CHAN_QWEST.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to specify the Qwest communication channel, supporting the system's ability to handle multiple channels distinctly and reliably.
CHAN_QWEST.name: CHAN_QWEST

usages.channels_8cc, void do_qwest(Character *c: Passed as an argument to specify that the communication should be sent through the qwest channel.

CHAN_PRAY.brief: Constant identifier for the prayer communication channel used in messaging functions.
CHAN_PRAY.details: The constant CHAN_PRAY is used to represent the prayer communication channel within the messaging system of the application. It is initialized with the value 3 and serves as a specific identifier when invoking functions such as 'channel' and 'do_pray', indicating that the message pertains to prayer communication. This allows the system to filter, route, and handle prayer messages separately from other communication channels, ensuring that only characters engaged in prayer or related activities process these messages appropriately.
CHAN_PRAY.notes: This constant is typically used as an argument in communication functions to specify the prayer channel, facilitating clear and consistent message handling for prayer-related interactions.
CHAN_PRAY.rationale: The CHAN_PRAY constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to identify the prayer communication channel within the messaging system, supporting organized and context-specific message delivery for characters engaging in prayer activities.
CHAN_PRAY.name: CHAN_PRAY

usages.channels_8cc, void do_pray(Character *ch: Serves as a constant identifier for the prayer communication channel, used as an argument in the 'channel' function to specify the type of channel.
usages.channels_8cc, void channel(Character *ch: Represents the pray channel, used to determine if messages should be sent or filtered for non-immortal characters.

CHAN_CLAN.brief: Constant representing the clan communication channel flag used for clan-specific messaging.
CHAN_CLAN.details: The CHAN_CLAN constant is used to identify the clan communication channel within the messaging system, enabling messages to be broadcast exclusively among members of the same clan. Initialized with the value 4, it functions as a bitmask flag that can be combined with other channel flags to specify the desired communication channel. This constant is commonly passed as an argument to functions such as channel() and do_clantalk(), which handle message routing and display, ensuring that clan messages are properly segregated from other communication channels like global or guild channels. Its consistent use facilitates organized and targeted clan interactions within the game environment.
CHAN_CLAN.notes: The constant is used as a bitmask flag, allowing for flexible combination with other channel flags if needed. Proper usage ensures clan messages are confined to clan members and do not leak into other channels.
CHAN_CLAN.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear and maintainable way to specify the clan communication channel, supporting organized clan interactions and preventing message leakage across different communication channels.
CHAN_CLAN.name: CHAN_CLAN

usages.channels_8cc, void do_clantalk(Character: Used as a parameter to specify that the communication should be sent through the clan channel.
usages.channels_8cc, void channel(Character *ch: Represents the clan channel, used to restrict message broadcasting to members of the same clan.

CHAN_MUSIC.brief: Constant representing the identifier for the music audio channel.
CHAN_MUSIC.details: The CHAN_MUSIC constant is an integer value used to uniquely identify the music channel within the audio system. It is typically used as an argument in functions that perform operations on specific channels, such as playing, stopping, or modifying music streams. By assigning a dedicated constant, the code maintains clarity and consistency when referencing the music channel, which is distinguished from other channels like sound effects or ambient audio. The value 5 is assigned based on the system's channel enumeration, ensuring proper integration with channel management routines.
CHAN_MUSIC.notes: This constant is often used in conjunction with other channel-related functions to target music-specific operations, ensuring that music streams are handled separately from other audio types.
CHAN_MUSIC.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to refer to the music channel, supporting organized audio management and preventing errors associated with hard-coded numeric values.
CHAN_MUSIC.name: CHAN_MUSIC

usages.channels_8cc, void do_music(Character *c: Used as a constant argument to specify that the channel function should operate on the music channel type.

CHAN_IC.brief: Constant representing the 'In Character' communication channel flag used for in-game chat messages.
CHAN_IC.details: The constant CHAN_IC is an integer value set to 6, representing the 'In Character' communication channel within the system. It is used as an argument in functions like channel() to specify that messages should be transmitted through the in-character chat channel, facilitating in-game roleplay communication. This constant helps the system distinguish between different communication channels, ensuring messages are routed correctly based on their designated flags. Its value aligns with the predefined channel identifiers used throughout the codebase, promoting consistency and clarity in channel management.
CHAN_IC.notes: Typically used as a flag in channel-related functions to indicate in-character communication. Its fixed value ensures compatibility with other channel flags and simplifies message routing logic.
CHAN_IC.rationale: The constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to specify the in-character communication channel, supporting the system's ability to handle multiple chat channels distinctly and efficiently.
CHAN_IC.name: CHAN_IC

usages.channels_8cc, void do_ic(Character *ch,: Used as an argument in the channel() function call to specify the communication channel for the message.

CHAN_GRATS.brief: An integer constant that identifies the 'grats' communication channel.
CHAN_GRATS.details: The constant CHAN_GRATS is used to represent the specific 'grats' channel within the messaging system, allowing messages to be routed appropriately. Initialized to the value 7, it is passed as an argument to channel-related functions to specify that the message should be sent through the 'grats' channel. This approach enhances code clarity by replacing hard-coded numeric values with a meaningful constant, facilitating easier maintenance and understanding of message routing logic.
CHAN_GRATS.notes: The constant is stored as a compile-time integer and is intended for use across various messaging functions to ensure consistent channel identification.
CHAN_GRATS.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to specify the 'grats' channel, supporting consistent message delivery and reducing errors associated with using raw numeric identifiers.
CHAN_GRATS.name: CHAN_GRATS

usages.channels_8cc, void do_grats(Character *c: Passed as an argument to the channel function to specify that the message should be sent through the 'grats' communication channel.

CHAN_IMMTALK.brief: Constant representing the 'immortal talk' communication channel used for communication among immortal characters.
CHAN_IMMTALK.details: The CHAN_IMMTALK constant is used to identify the dedicated communication channel for immortal characters within the system. Initialized with the value 8, it acts as a unique flag that distinguishes the immortal talk channel from other communication channels. This constant is employed in functions such as 'channel' and 'do_immtalk' to facilitate message transmission over the immortal channel, often involving special formatting, permissions, and visibility rules specific to immortal users. Its consistent use ensures clear and standardized referencing of the immortal communication pathway throughout the codebase.
CHAN_IMMTALK.notes: Typically stored as a bit flag within a set of channel flags, allowing for efficient permission checks and message routing for immortal characters.
CHAN_IMMTALK.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear and maintainable way to identify and handle immortal communication, ensuring that messages sent over this channel are appropriately formatted, permission-checked, and routed, thereby supporting the system's separation of communication privileges.
CHAN_IMMTALK.name: CHAN_IMMTALK

usages.channels_8cc, void do_immtalk(Character: Used as a parameter in the channel function call to specify the immtalk communication channel.
usages.channels_8cc, void channel(Character *ch: Represents the immortal talk channel, with special formatting and permission checks for immortals.

CHAN_QA.brief: A constant that identifies the question-and-answer (Q&A) communication channel.
CHAN_QA.details: The constant CHAN_QA is an integer value set to 9, used to represent the question-and-answer communication channel within the system. It is primarily employed as an argument in functions responsible for handling various communication channels, such as 'consumer_func', to specify that the message pertains to Q&A interactions. By using this constant, the code maintains clarity and consistency when distinguishing Q&A messages from other channel types, facilitating proper routing and handling of communication flows.
CHAN_QA.notes: This constant is typically stored as an integer flag and used in bitwise or direct comparisons to identify Q&A interactions. Its value is fixed and should not be altered to preserve compatibility across the codebase.
CHAN_QA.rationale: The CHAN_QA constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to specify the Q&A communication channel, enabling functions to correctly process and route Q&A messages without relying on magic numbers or hardcoded values.
CHAN_QA.name: CHAN_QA

usages.channels_8cc, void do_question(Character: Provided as an argument to channel to specify the question-and-answer communication channel.

CHAN_AUCTION.brief: A constant that identifies the auction communication channel within the system.
CHAN_AUCTION.details: The constant CHAN_AUCTION is used to specify the dedicated communication channel for auction-related messages in the application. Initialized with the value 10, it is referenced in functions like do_auction to broadcast or process auction notifications. This constant ensures that auction messages are correctly routed and handled separately from other communication channels, supporting organized message management across the system.
CHAN_AUCTION.notes: The constant is stored as an integer and is part of a set of channel identifiers, enabling efficient message filtering and processing based on channel type.
CHAN_AUCTION.rationale: This constant exists to provide a clear, maintainable way to identify and manage auction communication within the system, supporting features such as message broadcasting, logging, and channel-specific behavior.
CHAN_AUCTION.name: CHAN_AUCTION

usages._auction_8cc, void do_auction(Character: Constant representing the auction communication channel, used when broadcasting messages.
